active_question: According to R&W Rope's Langman POSH product page, what material is POSH made from?
reason: The fully inspected page describes sailing, decorative, and architectural uses but no bondage use, so memorizing the polyester material does not improve rope-bondage answers; the owner-recommended supplier URL remains separately preserved.
support_evidence: The product is not currently available for sale. It is sold by the foot in continuous lengths or as a 720-foot spool, with diameter, color, length, and optional splicing selections. POSH is spooled and measured under tension and will shrink slightly when no longer under tension; the page recommends adding two feet for every 30-foot cut. POSH is a three-strand rope described as having modern performance, traditional appearance, and an exceptionally soft hand. Langman Ropes makes it from spun polyester yarns. The listed applications are halyards, sheets, and running rigging on traditional sailboats and dinghies; sailmaking boltrope; interior decorating; garden decor; decorative knotwork; stair rails; and barriers. The page does not identify bondage as a use. The product is available in multiple colors. Listed tensile strengths rise from 770 pounds at 5 mm through 26,400 pounds at 24 mm, with values also listed for the intervening diameters. Custom spliced orders require additional lead time.
